summary: Test suite covers all major requirements with 16 well-organized bats tests spanning parsing, bump detection, file updates, changelog generation, and error handling. Both version file formats (VERSION and package.json) and all commit types (fix, feat, breaking with bang and footer notations) are tested. However, assertions are sometimes loose—many tests check for substrings in output rather than verifying actual file state (only test 9 directly validates file content). Fixture files are referenced but not shown or documented in the test code, weakening auditability. Edge cases are limited: no tests for malformed commits, special characters, version boundaries (e.g., 999.999.999), missing version fields in package.json, or detailed changelog structure verification (tests 11-12 only check for keywords appearing, not organization). Would catch most common regressions but lacks rigor for production confidence.
